name: Code Review workflow
description: Automated workflow: Code Review workflow. This workflow integrates 9 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, code, agent, githubTrigger. It contains 18 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: Code Review workflow workflow containing 14 nodes.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content: **1-The GitHub Trigger** node initiates the workflow whenever a pull request event occurs on a specified repository. It enables real-time automation based on GitHub activity.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
**2-Get PR Diffs**
The HTTP Request node fetches the list of changed files and their diffs from the pull request that triggered the workflow. It uses the GitHub REST API to retrieve this data dynamically based on the trigger payload.

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}{{$json.body.sender.login}}/{{$json.body.repository.name}}/pulls/{{$json.body.number}}/files

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
**3-Create Prompt from diffs**
This Code node runs a JavaScript snippet to:
-Parse file diffs from the previous HTTP Request node
-Format each diff with its file name
-Build a structured natural language prompt for the AI agent

The final output is a clear, contextual instruction like:
*"You are a senior iOS developer. Please review the following code changes in these files..."*

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
**Github Comment Poster**
Posts the AI-generated review as a comment on the pull request using GitHub API.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Code Review Agent
nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
**PR Labeler (optional)**
Automatically adds a label like *ReviewedByAI* to the pull request once the AI comment is posted.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content:
**Google Sheet Best Practices**
Enables the AI agent to reference to your team coding guidelines stored in a Google Sheet for more accurate and opinionated reviews.
You can replace Google Sheets with any other database or tool.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: Code Review workflow. This workflow integrates 9 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, code, agent, githubTrigger. It contains 18 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: Code Review workflow. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

nodes.name: Create target Prompt from PR Diffs

parameters.content: **1-The GitHub Trigger** node initiates the workflow whenever a pull request event occurs on a specified repository. It enables real-time automation based on GitHub activity.

**2-Get PR Diffs**
The HTTP Request node fetches the list of changed files and their diffs from the pull request that triggered the workflow. It uses the GitHub REST API to retrieve this data dynamically based on the trigger payload.

{{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}{{$json.body.sender.login}}/{{$json.body.repository.name}}/pulls/{{$json.body.number}}/files

**3-Create Prompt from diffs**
This Code node runs a JavaScript snippet to:
-Parse file diffs from the previous HTTP Request node
-Format each diff with its file name
-Build a structured natural language prompt for the AI agent

The final output is a clear, contextual instruction like:
*"You are a senior iOS developer. Please review the following code changes in these files..."*

**Github Comment Poster**
Posts the AI-generated review as a comment on the pull request using GitHub API.

**PR Labeler (optional)**
Automatically adds a label like *ReviewedByAI* to the pull request once the AI comment is posted.

**Google Sheet Best Practices**
Enables the AI agent to reference to your team coding guidelines stored in a Google Sheet for more accurate and opinionated reviews.
You can replace Google Sheets with any other database or tool.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
github  O Auth2  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "PR Trigger".
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get file's Diffs from PR" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "GitHub Robot" node (n8n-nodes-base.github) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Add Label to PR" node (n8n-nodes-base.github) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Code Best Practices" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Code Review Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
